steps.title: Substitute from the first equation
steps.content: The first equation already gives $y=-2x$. Substitute that into the second equation $3x+y=40$ instead of solving for both variables from scratch.

steps.title: Solve for $x$
steps.content: $$3x+(-2x)=40$$ $$x=40.$$ The intersection of the two lines also has x-coordinate $40$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The question asks for $x$, so the answer is 40. If needed, the corresponding $y$ would be $-80$, but that is not required.
